growth_records.cite: Corno G et al. Antibiotics promote aggregation within aquatic bacterial communities. Frontiers in Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2014.00297. [PMC4077313]
growth_records.snippet: The 9 vessels were filled with 750 ml of ALW medium enriched with 10 mg glucose L-1 as the sole C-source... in order to achieve a dilution rate of D = 0.2 d-1. The vessels were aerated from the bottom by fine bubbling with sterile air
growth_records.notes: Continuous-culture (chemostat) co-culture of 4 freshwater prokaryote strains, ALW + 10 mg/L glucose, 18+/-1 C, aerated. Dilution rate D = 0.2 /day = 0.2/24 = 0.00833 /h; at steady state mu = D = 0.00833 /h; doubling time = ln2/D = 0.693/0.00833 = 83.2 h (3.47 days). No per-substrate uptake/secretion rates measured (study measured cell abundance, community composition and aggregation only). NO-AB control condition; AB+ (12.5 ug/L) and AB++ (125 ug/L) treatments run at the same D (same steady-state mu) but community abundance dropped ~75% (to ~1 x 10^6 cells/mL). pH not stated. Growth rate is inferred from fixed dilution rate, not a direct physiological measurement.

carbon_growth.citation: Corno G et al. Antibiotics promote aggregation within aquatic bacterial communities. Frontiers in Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2014.00297. [PMC4077313]
